data_source: 10 completed match scorecards (04/18–06/28/2026) + season aggregate stats
match_approach: Jersey Aces are Group B's second-worst team — 0W/9L/1NR, 2 points, haven't won all season. This is a BONUS POINT game. Win the toss and BAT FIRST: push for 140–150+ to set a total that forces a big margin win. Then bowl them out under 80. WW are on a 5-game win streak with a settled unit — stick with the same lineup and take the NRR boost going into the tougher second half of the schedule.
form_notes: WW have won 5 consecutive matches (Princeton Walk, Plainsboro Hunters, MKCC Weekenders, PRO SQUAD, Troy Titans). Troy Titans were bowled out for 38 and chased in 7 overs — a textbook bonus-point demolition. Vinit Keni is the standout batter (174 runs, avg 29, back-to-back 45 and 55*). Ajinkya Pradhan is in elite form (21 wickets, econ 3.44). Siddhant Kumar broke through with 4 wickets vs Troy Titans. Amanraj Gupta is emerging as a clutch finisher (19* off 11 vs Troy Titans, 13* vs MKCC).

opponent_recent_form.analysis: Jersey Aces have not won a single game all season. They are second-bottom in Group B above only Monroe Giants Club (-2 pts). Their NRR of -2.91 reflects consistent heavy defeats. No specific player data available — but against a team this out of form, WW should treat this as a NRR-building exercise, not just a 4-point win.

batting_order.name: Vedant Kumar
batting_order.reason: The permanent opener. Three 20+ scores in 10 games provide the early platform. His 116+ SR in those big innings shows he attacks when set. Anomalous G10 (bat at #5 chasing 39) — ignore for order purposes.

batting_order.name: Yogen Phanse
batting_order.reason: Opens or bats #2 depending on lineup. Explosive SR (154–158 in his best outings) creates quick momentum. Also bowls containment in middle overs. Keep him early so his power-hitting phase overlaps with the powerplay.

batting_order.name: Vinit Keni
batting_order.reason: The cornerstone. When Keni stays in, WW win. His 45 and 55* in back-to-back games are the standout innings of the season. SR of 145 and 134 in those games — he is NOT slow. Protect him at #3 and build the innings around him. A big Keni innings today will set up the bonus point.

batting_order.name: Ajinkya Pradhan
batting_order.reason: Vice-captain and #1 bowler. Bats 4–6 depending on game state. His 20 Retired Hurt and 15 vs PRO SQUAD show he builds platforms. Bats here to conserve energy for full bowling quota.

batting_order.name: Anurag Dabee
batting_order.reason: Steady contributor who finishes games — 19* (PRO SQUAD) and 6* (Troy Titans) in the last two. Good between the wickets, reliable under pressure.

batting_order.name: Vivek Singh
batting_order.scorecard_form: EARLY ORDER (pos 3–4): 5,0,5,0,0,1 (6 innings). LOWER ORDER (pos 6–7): 11*(14), 52*(27) — 192 SR, 18(14). G10 opened chasing 39 (not meaningful).
batting_order.reason: CRITICAL: Vivek fails at top 4 (11 runs in 6 innings) and wins games from #6–7 (81 runs in 3 innings incl 52* off 27 balls vs Plainsboro). Float him to #5 only if WW are 50+ in 8 overs with wickets intact. Otherwise hold firm at 6–7.

batting_order.name: Amanraj Gupta
batting_order.reason: NEW INSIGHT: Amanraj has back-to-back match-finishing performances — 13* vs MKCC and 19* off 11 balls vs Troy Titans. His power hitting late in the innings is a genuine weapon. Move him up to #7 ahead of Shah to ensure he faces more balls.

batting_order.name: Malav Shah
batting_order.scorecard_form: 29(15) 4×6 SR=193 vs JC Warriors; 0(4); 10*(6) SR=167; 1(4); DNB vs PRO SQUAD; DNB vs Troy
batting_order.reason: The sixer machine at the death. When he gets deliveries in the final overs he hits them over the rope. Has not been dismissed much lately (DNBs) which means WW are finishing games before he bats — a sign of strong top/middle order performance.

batting_order.name: Siddhant Kumar
batting_order.reason: Useful lower-order bat (14* off 9 with 2 sixes vs MKCC). Bats low to preserve full bowling quota — after his 4-wicket haul vs Troy Titans he belongs in the lineup every game.

batting_order.name: Yuvraj Rajpurohit
batting_order.reason: 13 wickets at 4.22 economy across the season (Fnu + Yuvraj profiles combined). Bats low to preserve his full 4-over bowling allocation.

batting_order.name: Abhinav Tyagi
batting_order.reason: Pure bowling asset. Bats tail with licence to swing hard.

bowling_rotation.powerplay_note: PowerPlay = 4 overs. Captain can take it ANYTIME between overs 1–8 — this is a strategic weapon, not a fixed block. Best used when opposition's best batsmen are in to force errors under fielding restrictions.

bowlers.name: Ajinkya Pradhan
bowlers.match_data: Season: 21 wkts, econ 3.44. Last 3: 1.91 vs Troy (4wkts), 4.25 vs PRO SQUAD (1wkt), 3.00 vs MKCC (4wkts)
bowlers.reason: WW's best bowler by a distance. 21 wickets at 3.44 economy — elite. His Troy Titans spell (3.4-0-7-4, econ 1.91) was match-ending. Opens the attack and always finishes his 4-over quota.

bowlers.name: Namit Rathi
bowlers.match_data: Season econ 2.83; 1.00 econ vs Princeton Walk (3-1-3-2 — 2 wickets for 3 runs!)
bowlers.reason: The surprise PowerPlay weapon. Best economy in the squad. His Princeton Walk game (3 overs, 3 runs, 2 wickets) is the benchmark. Use him in every game he is available.

powerplay.alternative: If Namit Rathi is not playing, use Siddhant Kumar for a PowerPlay burst — his Troy Titans spell (4-0-12-4, econ 3.00) shows he can strike early.

bowlers.name: Malav Shah
bowlers.match_data: Last 4 full outings: 4.00 (Princeton Walk), 3.75 (Plainsboro), 3.67 (MKCC), 4.25 (PRO SQUAD), 4.00 (Troy Titans — 2 overs)
bowlers.reason: The containment workhorse. Economy between 3.67–4.25 in every recent outing — concedes under run-a-ball through the middle. His wickets are a bonus; his value is dot-ball pressure that forces errors for others.

bowlers.name: Yuvraj Rajpurohit
bowlers.match_data: Last 3: 4-0-11-2 (econ 2.75) vs Troy; 4-0-24-2 (econ 6.00) vs PRO SQUAD; 4-1-9-3 (econ 2.25) vs MKCC
bowlers.reason: 13 wickets across the season (two player profiles combined). Variable economy — excellent (2.25–2.75) when on, expensive (6.00) when off. Captain should watch his first 2 overs and rotate him out if leaking. Outstanding death option when in rhythm.

bowlers.name: Siddhant Kumar
bowlers.match_data: Last 3: 4-0-12-4 (econ 3.00) vs Troy Titans! 3-1-8-1 (econ 2.67) vs MKCC, 2.0-0-7-0 vs PRO SQUAD
bowlers.reason: UPGRADED: His 4-wicket haul vs Troy Titans (12 runs in 4 overs) is the best individual bowling performance of the season alongside Pradhan. Excellent in last two outings. Give him 3–4 overs now, not 2.

bowlers.name: Ajinkya Pradhan
bowlers.reason: Non-negotiable at the death. Slower ball and variation make him hardest to score off in overs 14–16. ALWAYS save his remaining overs for the death.

bowlers.name: Yuvraj Rajpurohit
bowlers.reason: Death specialist when his rhythm is right. The MKCC game (2.25 econ) and Troy Titans (2.75 econ) prove his quality. Pair with Pradhan for a two-pronged death attack.

bowlers.name: Abhinav Tyagi
bowlers.match_data: Season: 7 wkts, econ 6.50 — takes wickets but expensive
bowlers.reason: Raw pace can surprise at the death. Use as a 1-over option if the partnership needs breaking and both Pradhan/Yuvraj overs are done.

economy_first_bowlers.note: These bowlers build pressure through dot balls that lead to wickets for others. Updated after 10 matches.

bowlers.name: Ajinkya Pradhan
bowlers.note: Economy AND wickets — elite, best in squad by far

bowlers.name: Namit Rathi
bowlers.note: Best economy in squad — forces errors through dots

bowlers.name: Malav Shah
bowlers.note: Concedes under run-a-ball — crucial containment role

bowlers.name: Siddhant Kumar
bowlers.note: High wickets but economy variable — manage his overs carefully

bowlers.name: Yuvraj Rajpurohit
bowlers.note: Good wickets + economy when on — use full quota when in rhythm

key_matchups: Win the toss and BAT FIRST — against Jersey Aces, batting first and posting 140–150+ is the path to the bonus point (win by 60+ runs). Chasing a small total does nothing for NRR.
Ajinkya Pradhan bowls his FULL 4-over quota — 21 wickets at 3.44 economy is the bedrock. Use 2 overs up front in the PowerPlay, 2 at death. Non-negotiable.
Siddhant Kumar has earned more overs — his 4-wicket haul vs Troy Titans (4-0-12-4) is the best bowling performance WW have had from him all season. Give him 3–4 overs now.

Vivek Singh MUST bat 6 or 7 — his top-order record (11 runs in 6 innings) versus lower-order record (81 runs in 3 innings incl. 52* off 27) is night and day. Do not be tempted to promote him just because the opposition is weak.
Vinit Keni should anchor and bat big — a 40+ from Keni sets up 140+. Build the innings around him at #3.
Amanraj Gupta at #7 — his last two innings (13* vs MKCC, 19* off 11 vs Troy Titans) show he is a genuine finisher. He should bat ahead of Shah to guarantee more balls.

scorecard_insights.vivek_singh_pattern: Positions 3–4: 5,0,5,0,0,1 = 11 runs in 6 innings. Positions 6–7: 11*,52*,18 = 81 runs in 3 innings. Data is conclusive — he wins games from down the order.
scorecard_insights.vinit_keni_anchor: When Keni bats 20+ balls, WW win. 45(31) and 55*(41) in the last two batting-first games anchored totals of 122 and 111. Non-negotiable at #3.
scorecard_insights.pradhan_dominance: 10 games, 21 wickets, econ 3.44. Best spells: 1.00 vs Em Sports, 1.50 vs Princeton Walk, 1.91 vs Troy Titans, 2.33 vs Plainsboro, 3.00 vs MKCC. There is no better bowler in this squad — or arguably in the league.
scorecard_insights.siddhant_breakthrough: Troy Titans: 4-0-12-4 (econ 3.00). MKCC: 3-1-8-1 (econ 2.67). Siddhant has two consecutive excellent spells after months of inconsistency. He should now start with 3–4 overs, not 2.
scorecard_insights.amanraj_finisher: NEW: Amanraj Gupta has 19*(11, SR=173) vs Troy Titans and 13*(15) vs MKCC as a #6–7. He is becoming the clutch finisher WW needed behind Malav Shah. Bat him 7th.
scorecard_insights.rathi_powerplay_weapon: Princeton Walk: 3-1-3-2 (econ 1.00). Rathi in the PowerPlay is a genuine game-changer. Use him every match he plays.
scorecard_insights.malav_shah_containment: Last 5 bowling outings: 4.00, 3.75, 3.67, 4.25, 4.00 econ — the most consistent containment bowler in the squad after Pradhan.
scorecard_insights.collapse_warning: Monroe Mavericks (G4): WW collapsed to 21 all out in 9.1 overs. That fragility still lives in this batting order when the top 3 fall early. Protect Keni at all costs — the lower order cannot rescue a 3-wicket collapse without him.
